title: Merch This Asuka figure seems hard to find
display_title: Merch This Asuka figure seems hard to find
wikitext:
[]: [Merch] This Asuka figure seems hard to find...             Quote Post by Ender » Wed Feb 12, 2014 6:08 pm Here's the only reference picture I've found so far: http://imgur.com/5ED0a2w I've scoured myfigurecollection.net for it, I've looked through hundreds of listings to no avail. I found this pic posted by another forum member, shaneva002. I messaged him, but his profile says he hasn't been on since September. I have over 40 Asuka figures, I'm on a quest to gather a figure for each canon outfit she's worn. However this particular figure is just too unique to say "Oh it's just another plug suit", plus I like a challenge. Thanks in advance! Here's most of my collection so far: http://imgur.com/a/93aTF#0

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed Feb 12, 2014 6:27 pm Being a collector is suffering.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Feb 13, 2014 12:49 am ^Once you get on that road, you gotta deal with it or get off. This is a tough one, even Tolwyn's http://evangelion.eu/ doesn't have that figure and his overall collection is humongous. Ender wrote: Here's most of my collection so far

[]:             Quote Post by Ender » Thu Feb 13, 2014 1:08 am Someone found me more info on the figure! http://www.e2046.com/product/4304 I made both the Lego NERV logo and the bag (it's a laptop sleeve). I ordered custom print fabric from spoonflower.com and used my badass sewing machine to make it. You know Husqvarna, the company that makes world renowned chainsaws? Turns out they make tough-as-nails sewing machines, too.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Feb 13, 2014 4:00 pm My god those Asuka figs. I aspire to one day have a collection that big. Also very nice choice of Daki. I'm thinking of either getting that one or the Clesta one next time I buy one.

[]: Re: This Asuka figure seems hard to find...             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Feb 13, 2014 4:05 pm Ender wrote: Here's most of my collection so far: http://imgur.com/a/93aTF#0

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Feb 13, 2014 4:43 pm Ender wrote: Someone found me more info on the figure! http://www.e2046.com/product/4304

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Feb 13, 2014 7:27 pm ^Thought something was odd when I saw how poor quality their pic of that figure was. They even admit that they're now based in China. CJD wrote: the Clesta one

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Feb 13, 2014 7:57 pm They've always been based in Hong Kong. The site name, E2046 refers to the year when Hong Kong's special status expires EDIT: I keep getting distracted whenever e2046 is brought up. I've actully check E-Mono, Eva Figure Archive, Eva 3D books and the merch sections of the Chronicles Essentials books and that Asuka figure isn't in there. The first three usually cover the majority of the pre-rebuild merch so it's strange not to find it there. Either this fig is newer or from a really obscure sculptor.

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Feb 13, 2014 11:20 pm It's a garage kit. They're fan-made resin casts sold at Japanese events that you have to clean, assemble, and paint yourself. Even if you were able to find the kit, which is probably impossible now, you would need serious modeling know-how to complete it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ender » Fri Feb 14, 2014 12:24 am ^^^ I hire Leona's Workshop to do my custom figures (just one so far, but I'd like to have her do more). I think she does quality work. TMBounty_Hunter wrote: E2046 are bootleggers. They're physical pirates. They try to acquire any and all figure and resin kits made then recast them on demand. This is why thier site is 90% pre-orders. They just put the page up to gather interest and guage if it's profitable for them to try and get a hold of the original. This is why you'll never find original sculptor information there, making the site mostly useless. You'll get a picture but usually that's the end of the trail.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Feb 14, 2014 8:59 am Killer Bee wrote: you would need serious modeling know-how to complete it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ender » Fri Feb 14, 2014 6:08 pm wooo! But... I don't know Japanese... how might I bid on this?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Feb 14, 2014 6:44 pm Since most Y!J sellers don't ship internationally there are quite a few proxy services that will buy this and ship it to you, for a fee of course. Since I've never used any I don't know which to recommend. Those yens are gonna add up...

[]:             Quote Post by Ender » Fri Feb 14, 2014 7:05 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Those yens are gonna add up...

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sat Feb 15, 2014 1:34 am I know someone in Japan who I met through ebay who's helped me place orders through the Eva-T store when that was still up and make some bids on YJA. Her fee is 10% of item price, you cover shipping and fees (bank transfer in this case to pay the seller and Paypal). I can send her your email if you let me know by PM. However the auction ends on the 16th and there will be lags in response time given the time difference. There are currently no bids, so the seller could relist the kit if no one else picks it up. If you want faster turnaround you could go with the proxies but I don't know how long their set up process might take. I'd say get in touch with her anyway and if the kit is relisted or is found elsewhere then you are in business.

[]:             Quote Post by Ender » Sat Feb 15, 2014 3:11 pm I'm using this site: http://www.fromjapan.co.jp/ It's working out so far and I've heard good things. I got a bid in and found some other cool stuff, too.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Feb 17, 2014 3:42 pm Good luck next time.
